summary: P05 centralized Darwin/Linux platform differences behind adapter interfaces and added dry-run doctor capability reporting without real external probes.
expected_outputs: Methods: detect_platform, check_port_available, process_exists, read_process_rss, count_process_fds, list_sockets, supports_host_network, supports_network_fault_injection, network_fault_backend_hint
doctor --dry-run can report capabilities through adapter without changing system state

verified_outputs.evidence: harness/executor.py and unit test fake executor assertions

verified_outputs.output: Methods: detect_platform, check_port_available, process_exists, read_process_rss, count_process_fds, list_sockets, supports_host_network, supports_network_fault_injection, network_fault_backend_hint
verified_outputs.evidence: adapter interface and platform-specific implementations compile and tests cover capabilities

verified_outputs.output: doctor --dry-run can report capabilities through adapter without changing system state
verified_outputs.evidence: python3 -m harness.harnessctl doctor --dry-run --json exit_code=0 and unit test checks platform_capabilities

commands.command: python3 -m py_compile harness/platform_adapter.py harness/platform_darwin.py harness/platform_linux.py harness/executor.py harness/harnessctl.py

risks: Real lsof/ps/docker/tc behavior is not validated in P05.
Darwin network fault support remains explicitly unsupported.
